academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent with a minimum three-year undergraduate background in Civil Engineering or related subjects, together with proven competencies across core civil-engineering and quantitative areas. Country-based entry-title and GPA requirements also apply, and selection is based on academic record and curricular fit.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor's degree or equivalent; minimum three-year undergraduate degree in Civil Engineering or related subjects, to be obtained by 31 July 2026 for the EU and EU-equated foreign-qualification call.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must show competencies in Hydraulics; Hydraulic and marine constructions and hydrology; Geotechnics; Structural mechanics; Structural engineering; Highways, railways and airports; Transportation; Topography and cartography; Building design; Drawing; Urban and regional planning; Mathematical physics; Numerical analysis; Statistics; Thermal engineering and industrial energy systems; Building physics and building energy systems; Materials science and technology; Chemical foundations of technologies.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-based specific entry-title and GPA requirements apply on the official University of Padua entry-title page. For the EU and EU-equated foreign-qualification call, ranking is based on final GPA or the average grade of exams taken, together with the relevance of the entry qualification and completed exams; candidates must score at least 60/100 to be included in the ranking.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 (CEFR) or equivalent certificate, to be achieved by 31 July 2026 for the EU and EU-equated foreign-qualification call.
accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum score 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First: minimum score 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum score 126.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum score 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if their previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua.
language_requirements.deadline: 31 July 2026 for the EU and EU-equated foreign-qualification call.

application_deadline_eu.notes: Call two is a second round only if places remain unfilled after the first ranking list is exhausted.

application_deadline_non_eu.notes: This call is for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy. The programme page does not state a further non-EU foreign-qualification round.

required_documents.document_name: Academic transcript of records and bachelor's degree diploma if already obtained
required_documents.notes: The transcript must include the full list of exams taken, the grades obtained, and the grading scale.

required_documents.document_name: Official translations into Italian or English
required_documents.notes: Required if academic documents are issued only in a language other than Italian or English.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate of at least B2 level
required_documents.notes: If not possessed at application stage, it must be achieved and submitted by 31 July 2026.
The official programme evaluation-form template must be used.
Both documents are required as part of the application package.

required_documents.document_name: Legalisation or Apostille or CIMEA Statement of Verification
required_documents.notes: If admitted, one of these forms of verification must be submitted by 31 October 2026. A CIMEA Statement of Comparability may also be requested if further verification is needed.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and study visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and obtain the study visa for enrolment.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No written entrance exam; ranking-based selection with evaluation form and possible online interview
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants are assessed on academic transcript, curricular fit, and the evaluation form; only candidates requested by the committee may be invited to an online interview.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No fixed interview schedule is published; any online interview is arranged during the selection procedure.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The ranking is drawn up on a 100-point basis considering final GPA or average grade of exams taken and the relevance of the entry qualification and completed exams. Candidates must score at least 60/100 to be included in the ranking.

source_quotes.quote: This is a Limited access programme.

A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Civil Engineering or related subjects, with proven skills in Hydraulics; Hydraulic and marine constructions and hydrology; Geotechnics; Structural mechanics; Structural engineering; Highways, railways and airports; Transportation; Topography and cartography; Building design; Drawing; Urban and regional planning; Mathematical physics; Numerical analysis; Statistics; Thermal engineering and industrial energy systems; Building physics and building energy systems; Materials science and technology; Chemical foundations of technologies.
English language: B2 level (CEFR) or equivalent to be achieved by July 31, 2026.
Call for non-EU students: 7 January 2026 – 7 March 2026
it is necessary to achieve a total score higher than or equal to 60/100.

uncertainty_notes: The official foreign-qualification call states that the programme activation is subject to accreditation under Ministerial Decree 1154/2021.
The English call is provided for informational purposes; when disputes arise, the official Italian version has legal validity.
